title: FabricationAssemblyPlanningInterfaces
description: Shared cross-runtime JSON payloads for hybrid fabrication assembly planning workers that split, combine, join, sequence, and learn from printed, milled, turned, sheet-cut, and postprocessed parts. NATS subject names live in @dd/nats-subject-defs; these types describe the request and result bodies published on those subjects.

FabricationAssemblySourceArtifact.title: FabricationAssemblySourceArtifact
FabricationAssemblySourceArtifact.description: Verified source artifact that can drive part splitting, joining, assembly graph generation, process sequencing, or learning-state updates.

artifactId.description: Stable artifact id retained by the planner, usually from CAD conversion or instruction generation.

format.description: Artifact format such as STEP, STL, 3MF, DXF, ASSEMBLY_GRAPH_JSON, CAM_SETUP_JSON, SHEET_NESTING_JSON, GCODE, NC, SETUP_SHEET_MD, or INSPECTION_PLAN_JSON.

uri.description: Sanitized artifact URI or object key. Do not include credentials, query strings, or fragments.

sha256.description: SHA-256 of the source artifact when available.

partIds.description: Part or subassembly ids represented by this artifact.

evidence.description: Evidence labels already attached to this artifact, such as units-verified, mesh-manifold, cam-setup-reviewed, toolpath-simulated, or inspection-plan-reviewed.

FabricationAssemblyMachineCapability.title: FabricationAssemblyMachineCapability
FabricationAssemblyMachineCapability.description: Available fabrication or postprocess capability that can be used when splitting or combining parts.

capabilityId.description: Stable capability id, e.g. prusa-mk4-fdm, haas-vf2-mill, okuma-lathe, laser-sheet, heat-set-insert-station, or inspection-cmm.

machineClass.description: Machine or process class such as additive-fdm, additive-resin, vertical-mill, horizontal-mill, lathe, router, laser, waterjet, plasma, postprocess-station, inspection, or assembly.

materials.description: Material or stock families supported by this capability.

limits.description: Human-readable limits such as build volume, travel, chuck diameter, sheet thickness, tolerance band, surface finish, or operator-only steps.

requiredEvidence.description: Evidence labels required before this capability can be included in a machine-ready plan.

FabricationAssemblyCandidatePart.title: FabricationAssemblyCandidatePart
FabricationAssemblyCandidatePart.description: One proposed part, subpart, insert, fixture-dependent feature group, or subassembly in a hybrid fabrication plan.

partId.description: Stable proposed part or subassembly id.

parentPartId.description: Parent object or source part id when this candidate is split from a larger part.

role.description: Design role such as printed-shell, milled-insert, turned-shaft, sheet-cover, fixture, support, sacrificial-tab, postprocess-feature, or final-assembly.

preferredProcess.description: Preferred process such as fdm-print, resin-print, vertical-mill, horizontal-mill, lathe-turning, sheet-cut, waterjet-cut, heat-set-inserts, bonding, pressing, fastening, inspection, or assembly.

material.description: Requested or proposed material for this part.

sourceArtifactIds.description: Source artifact ids used to derive this candidate part.

rationale.description: Short reason this part should be separate, combined, printed, milled, turned, sheet-cut, or postprocessed.

FabricationAssemblyJoinInterface.title: FabricationAssemblyJoinInterface
FabricationAssemblyJoinInterface.description: One join, datum, mating surface, press-fit, fastener pattern, adhesive joint, heat-set insert, weld, or operator alignment interface.

fromPartId.description: First part id in the interface.

toPartId.description: Second part id in the interface.

joinMethod.description: Join method such as fastener, heat-set-insert, press-fit, adhesive, weld, dovetail, alignment-pin, bearing-fit, slip-fit, operator-fixture, or inspect-only.

datumOrFit.description: Datum, tolerance, clearance, interference, or fit summary.

requiredEvidence.description: Evidence required before this join can be released, such as tolerance-stack-reviewed, joint-strength-verified, operator-alignment-verified, adhesive-cure-verified, or inspection-datum-verified.

FabricationAssemblyProcessStep.title: FabricationAssemblyProcessStep
FabricationAssemblyProcessStep.description: One generated operation, inspection, postprocess, or assembly sequencing step proposed by an assembly planner.

sequence.description: Process order within the plan candidate.

process.description: Process label such as fdm-print, vertical-mill, lathe-turning, sheet-cut, deburr, heat-set-inserts, adhesive-cure, inspection, or final-assembly.

partIds.description: Part ids affected by this step.

capabilityId.description: Capability id used by this step when known.

operatorIntervention.description: Human action, handoff, setup, fixture, measurement, or approval needed at this step.

outputArtifactTargets.description: Instruction-generation targets or artifact labels expected from this step.

FabricationAssemblyBlocker.title: FabricationAssemblyBlocker
FabricationAssemblyBlocker.description: A blocker discovered while splitting, combining, joining, sequencing, or learning from a hybrid fabrication plan.

code.description: Stable blocker code, e.g. split-boundary-unverified, combine-before-release-required, separate-piece-required, joint-strength-verification-required, tolerance-stack-review-required, datum-transfer-required, human-intervention-required, or learning-outcome-required.

message.description: Human-readable blocker summary safe for operator logs.

partId.description: Part id associated with this blocker when applicable.

interfaceId.description: Join/interface id associated with this blocker when applicable.

stepId.description: Process step id associated with this blocker when applicable.

machineReadyImpact.description: Effect on release posture, e.g. blocks-machine-ready, needs-operator-review, warning-only, or learning-only.

FabricationAssemblyPlanCandidate.title: FabricationAssemblyPlanCandidate
FabricationAssemblyPlanCandidate.description: One proposed hybrid assembly and process decomposition plan.

strategy.description: High-level strategy such as split-for-print-and-machine, combine-printed-shell-with-milled-inserts, separate-for-support-removal, merge-before-machining, or keep-monolithic.

score.description: Planner or worker score for ranking candidates. Larger is better within one result envelope.

machineReady.description: True only when this specific candidate has enough assembly evidence for machine-ready release consideration.

parts.description: Candidate parts and subassemblies in this plan.

interfaces.description: Join, datum, fit, fastener, or operator alignment interfaces in this plan.

processSteps.description: Ordered process sequence for this plan.

FabricationAssemblyLearningSignal.title: FabricationAssemblyLearningSignal
FabricationAssemblyLearningSignal.description: Compact MDP/POMDP or neural-learning hint emitted by an assembly worker for future outcome attribution.

signalKind.description: Learning signal kind such as mdp-state, pomdp-observation, policy-action, reward-hint, failure-boundary, split-combine-decision, or outcome-label.

stateFeatures.description: Compact state features such as material-process mix, tolerance stack, machine class, join method, support removal, operator intervention, or failure boundary.

observations.description: POMDP observations, uncertainty labels, sensor hints, or human-review observations.

recommendedAction.description: Recommended policy action such as split-part, combine-parts, add-inspection-step, request-human-review, or reroute-process.

rewardHint.description: Optional reward hint for policy learning.

FabricationAssemblyPlanningRequest.title: FabricationAssemblyPlanningRequest
FabricationAssemblyPlanningRequest.description: Body published on dd.remote.fabrication.assembly.planning.requests for hybrid assembly and process-decomposition workers.

schema.description: Envelope schema id. Producers should emit dd.fabrication.assembly-planning.request.v1.

requestId.description: Unique id for this assembly planning job, suitable for dedupe and result correlation.

planRequestId.description: Fabrication planning request id that spawned this assembly planning job.

jobId.description: Optional fabrication job id when the planner has already persisted one.

emittedAtMs.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when the planner published this request.

producer.description: Producer service instance or deployment name.

resultSubject.description: Subject workers should publish the result to; defaults to dd.remote.fabrication.assembly.planning.results.

objective.description: Human-readable fabrication objective, including whether the worker should split, combine, sequence, validate, or learn from candidate process plans.

sourceArtifacts.description: Source artifacts available to the worker.

capabilities.description: Available fabrication, postprocess, inspection, and assembly capabilities.

candidateParts.description: Initial part and subassembly candidates from the planner.

constraints.description: Design, tolerance, material, machine, schedule, cost, learning, or operator constraints.

blockers.description: Planner-known blockers that workers may clear by returning split/combine plans, joins, process sequences, or learning evidence.

priority.description: Optional scheduling hint. Higher values mean more urgent assembly planning work.

notes.description: Non-secret planning notes for the worker.

FabricationAssemblyPlanningResult.title: FabricationAssemblyPlanningResult
FabricationAssemblyPlanningResult.description: Body published on dd.remote.fabrication.assembly.planning.results with hybrid part decomposition, combine/split decisions, joins, process sequence, learning hints, blockers, and machine-ready posture.

schema.description: Envelope schema id. Workers should emit dd.fabrication.assembly-planning.result.v1.

requestId.description: Assembly planning request id this result answers.

planRequestId.description: Fabrication planning request id from the original assembly planning job.

jobId.description: Optional persisted fabrication job id from the original request.

emittedAtMs.description: Unix epoch milliseconds when the worker published this result.

workerId.description: Worker id, pod name, or adapter instance that produced this result.

planner.description: Assembly planner, optimizer, policy model, or adapter used.

plannerVersion.description: Planner or model version used for repeatability and learning attribution.

success.description: True when the worker completed its requested assembly planning or review work.

machineReady.description: True only when returned candidates and evidence are sufficient for the planner to consider the assembly lane releasable.

selectedPlanId.description: Best candidate id selected by the worker, when one can be recommended.

candidates.description: Generated hybrid assembly and process decomposition candidates.

learningSignals.description: Compact MDP/POMDP/neural-learning hints retained for outcome attribution.

blockers.description: Remaining blockers or new worker-discovered blockers.

warnings.description: Non-blocking warnings that should be retained with the assembly evidence.

reviewMetadata.description: Free-form non-secret metadata such as model confidence, process graph ids, policy names, cost estimates, tolerance-stack summaries, or operator-review notes.
